thesis_claim: The 'split mind' folk-model is the exact inverse of the mechanism — this is a failure of integration, not a multiplication of selves.
the_risk: The dopamine hypothesis, taught as THE mechanism, is alone insufficient; glutamate/NMDA hypofunction is co-equal; no biomarker, no single gene, no one chain — converging partial accounts.
p3_chart_decision: Hazard 2: the 'contributing-factor weighting' ArticleChart is NOT shipped as a single commensurable chart (twin heritability, GWAS loci, adversity OR, cannabis OR are different study designs and not comparable). Reallocated to a second DiagramBlock (a convergence map) — decided at data pass, logged in RUN_REPORT.

block_plan.content: Why does this illness wait until late adolescence and early adulthood, and almost never arrive in childhood? (State the onset timing qualitatively — the 20.5y number is owned by Part 1; here it is the WHY.)
The neurodevelopmental account: the insult is early, the expression is late; symptoms appear when affected regions mature.

steps: Adolescent synaptic pruning — prefrontal remodeling continues into the third decade (Petanjek); complement C4 mediates synapse elimination and its over-expression raises risk (Sekar)
Dopamine — presynaptic striatal dysregulation drives aberrant salience (Howes 2012: presynaptic d=0.79; version III, Howes-Kapur 2009)
Glutamate / NMDA hypofunction — a co-equal, non-dopaminergic account; NMDA antagonists reproduce positive AND negative symptoms (Moghaddam; Krystal ketamine)
Environment — childhood adversity (OR 2.78), cannabis dose-response (OR 3.90), urbanicity (2.37x), migration (RR 2.7)

block_plan.content: Twin heritability ~79-81%, but MZ concordance only 33% — genes are not destiny. GWAS: 287 loci, no single gene — the absence of one is itself the finding.
Pathway: early genetic/developmental risk -> adolescent pruning + C4 -> presynaptic dopamine dysregulation -> aberrant salience -> psychosis; environment modulates throughout. Include a11y_summary.

block_plan.note: REPLACES the dishonest factor-weighting chart (Hazard 2). Shows the four converging partial accounts (genetic/polygenic, developmental/pruning, neurochemical dopamine+glutamate, environmental) feeding one outcome, WITHOUT plotting incommensurable effect sizes on one axis. a11y_summary required; caption states 'different study designs; effect sizes not directly comparable'.

block_plan.content: No biomarker. No diagnostic test. The mechanism is a set of converging partial accounts, not one chain — and saying so is more interesting than a hedge (Kapur 2012).

block_plan.myth: Cold or distant mothering causes schizophrenia (the 'schizophrenogenic mother').
block_plan.fact: A real mid-20th-century theory, since discredited by its own former promulgators as a psychiatric myth — parenting does not cause schizophrenia.

block_plan.myth: There is a single 'schizophrenia gene', or it is purely genetic.
block_plan.fact: Risk is spread across at least 287 genomic loci with no single causal gene; genotype and environment interact (COMT × cannabis), and identical-twin concordance is only ~33%.

block_plan.content: Accordion unpacking each mechanism for the curious reader.
